skill.name: zone-prescription
skill.purpose:
Generate variable-rate prescriptions for seed, nitrogen, and/or fertilizer with zone-level map visualization and downloadable ISOXML files.
Use when: grower wants variable-rate prescriptions, asks to optimize seeding/nitrogen/fertilizer rates by zone, needs a prescription file for equipment (John Deere Operations Center, Climate FieldView, etc.), or wants to see rate recommendations on a map.

parameters.param: Comma-separated list of prescription types to generate. Options: seed, nitrogen, fertilizer. Ask the user which type(s) they want. If unclear, default to "seed". Multiple types can be combined: "seed,nitrogen" generates both.
Company ID. REQUIRED for performance — without it the query scans the entire database and will timeout.
Optional filter to a single field. Omit for all fields in the company.

interpretation.stage:
Seeding rate prescriptions use drainage-stratified yield response data. For each drainage class, the population bucket with the highest weighted-average yield becomes the recommended rate. This is empirical, not theoretical — derived from THIS operation's actual data.
Zones with rate change less than 500 seeds/ac (corn) or 3,000 seeds/ac (soybeans) are treated as "on target."

Nitrogen prescriptions use application history correlated with yield response. Zones are grouped by soil productivity tier and drainage class. The N rate that produced peak yield in each group becomes the recommendation. If no application data exists, soil-productivity-based defaults are used.

Fertilizer prescriptions use yield-based nutrient removal rates adjusted by soil productivity. Higher-yielding zones need more maintenance fertility. Soil test data (if available) adjusts recommendations by current nutrient levels.

skill.communication:
Present results as actionable: "I've generated [type] prescriptions for your [crop]. The zone map shows recommended rates across [N] zones ([M] acres). The ISOXML file is ready to download and load into your planter/applicator display."
When multiple prescription types are combined: "I've generated seed and nitrogen prescriptions. Use the layer switcher on the zone map to toggle between seeding rate and N rate views. Both prescriptions are included in the download file."
If most zones show no change from current rates, frame positively: "Your current rates are well-matched. Only [X] zones have meaningful adjustments — the rest are on target."
Never present rates as universal truth — they're derived from this operation's specific data and conditions.
skill.follow_up: After prescription generation, offer: - "Want me to run the YP1K analysis for a deeper look at hybrid-level seeding rate efficiency?" - "Should I export this as a shapefile for use in other GIS tools?" - "Want to see how these rates compare to last year's application?" - "You can fork this skill and customize the rate logic for your specific agronomic approach — just ask me to help you modify it."
